methodology.canonical_text_recipe: Sort canonical chapters/*.json lexicographically; read content, else text, else body; strip each non-empty chapter; join with two LF characters; strip the joined value; append one LF; SHA-256 the UTF-8 bytes. Character count is Unicode code points in that exact value.
methodology.canonical_source_selection: Use root data when root/backend manuscript hashes match. Use the complete backend packet when the root packet has no chapters. For Dracula, use the clean backend packet because the root packet contains raw HTML. Retain root precedence for Frankenstein but block audition because the complete backend packet has a different manuscript.
methodology.manifest_rule: Report tracked book-import-manifest inclusion separately from controlled reader-manifest inclusion. Lack of a tracked import-manifest row is not by itself an audition blocker when the controlled packet has matching chapters plus Tier A source/approval evidence; current release factory inventory has a controlled-publication fallback.
methodology.cover_rule: Pass direct non-placeholder front/back URLs or explicit graphical runtime fallback evidence. Newer title-specific Sprint1 cover blockers override the older generic fallback report.
methodology.sanitation_rule: Require matrix PASS, reader_facing_boilerplate_removed=true, and a clean local scan of the selected canonical manuscript. Matrix links to nonexistent sanitized_text_reports were not treated as evidence.
methodology.readiness_rule: ready_for_audition means all non-paid input gates pass and a provider audition is the next safe stage. It is false for already-approved audio, reuse-first/non-paid preflights, an already-passed representative audition, unresolved public-asset containment, source-loader/path conflicts, or decommissioned attempts.

titles.sanitation_review_note: The standalone Bengali digit ২ in chapter-040 is a numbered literary section marker between verse stanzas, not page boilerplate.
